title: The One Where Ross Finds Out

expressions.dialogue:
Rach: So, how was the party?
Chan: Well it couldn't have been worse. A woman literally passed through me. OK, so what is it, am I hideously unattractive?
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 누군가가 자신을 존재하지 않는 사람처럼 완전히 무시하고 지나쳤다
expressions.nuance: 물리적으로 '통과한다'는 표현으로 투명인간 취급을 과장해서 묘사. 'literally'와 함께 쓰면 자기비하 유머가 극대화된다.
expressions.example: 파티에서 짝사랑 상대가 내 존재를 완전히 무시하고 지나갔을 때 → 'She literally passed right through me. I might as well have been a ghost.'

expressions.dialogue: Mnca: Chandler, I'm unemployed and in dire need of a project. Ya wanna work out? I can remake you.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: ~가 절박하게 필요한 상태다
expressions.nuance: 'Dire'는 원래 극도로 심각한 상황에 쓰는 단어인데 여기선 유머러스하게 과장해서 씀. 진지한 맥락과 가벼운 맥락 모두 가능하다.
expressions.example: 마감 직전에 커피가 떨어졌을 때 → 'I'm in dire need of coffee right now. This report won't write itself.'

expressions.dialogue:
Mnca: Chandler, I'm unemployed and in dire need of a project. Ya wanna work out? I can remake you.
Chan: Oh, you know, I would, but that might get in the way of my lying around time.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 아무것도 안 하고 늘어져 있는 시간
expressions.nuance: 실제로 존재하는 중요한 일정인 것처럼 말해서 게으름을 정당화하는 유머. 'get in the way of'와 세트로 쓰면 효과가 배가된다.
expressions.example: 주말에 친구가 뭔가 하자고 부를 때 → 'Sorry, that's gonna cut into my lying around time. I had a very packed schedule of doing nothing.'

expressions.dialogue: Phoe: No, not weight... y'know, more like insulation.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: (~라기보다는) 차라리 ~에 가깝다, 오히려 ~라고 하는 게 맞다
expressions.nuance: 앞서 한 말이나 상대방의 말을 살짝 수정·재포장할 때 씀. 여기선 '살쪘다'는 말 대신 '단열재(보온층)'라는 완곡어로 슬쩍 바꾸는 게 포인트.
expressions.example: 친구가 '넌 굉장히 꼼꼼하네'라고 했을 때 → 'Nah, more like obsessive. I reorganized my closet three times this week.'

expressions.dialogue:
Rach: Hey Phoebs, how'd it go with Scott last night?
Phoe: Oh, um, it was nice. Took him to a romantic restaurant, ordered champagne, nice.
Joey: The guy still won't put out, huh?
Phoe: Nope. Zilch, nothin', uh-uh.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 성관계를 거부한다, 잠자리를 안 해준다
expressions.nuance: 다소 직접적인 구어 속어. 파트너가 성적으로 응해주지 않을 때 씀. 친한 친구 사이에서는 자연스럽게 등장하는 표현이다.
expressions.example: 오래 사귀었는데 관계 진전이 없다고 친구에게 털어놓을 때 → 'We've been dating for months and he still won't put out. I genuinely don't know what's going on.'

expressions.expression: drives his car on the other side of the road
expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Maybe he, uhh... drives his car on the other side of the road, if ya know what I mean.
Phoe: No, whad'ya mean? He's not British.
Joey: Maybe he's... gay.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 게이일 수 있다는 것을 돌려 말하는 완곡 표현
expressions.nuance: 영국의 좌측통행을 빗댄 비유. Phoebe가 글자 그대로 해석하는 데서 웃음이 터지는 구조. 완곡어법이 얼마나 모호할 수 있는지 보여주는 전형적 예시.
expressions.example: 이런 완곡 표현이 전혀 안 통했을 때 → 'I dropped the whole "drives on the other side" hint and she had absolutely no idea what I meant.'

expressions.dialogue:
Chan: So you really OK about all this?
Rach: Oh yeah, c'mon, I'm movin' on.
...
Rach: Oh what, my whole insane jealousy thing? Well, y'know, as much fun as that was, I've decided to opt for sanity.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 이성적이고 건강한 선택을 하기로 결정했다
expressions.nuance: 'Sanity'(제정신)를 선택 가능한 옵션처럼 표현해서 자신의 비이성적 상태를 자조적으로 인정하면서 전환을 선언하는 표현.
expressions.example: 오랫동안 스트레스 주는 사람에게서 거리를 뒀을 때 → 'After months of overthinking every text, I decided to opt for sanity and just let it go.'

expressions.dialogue: Rach: He can press her up against that window as much as he wants. For all I care, he can throw her through the damn thing.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 누군가를 ~에 바짝 밀어붙이다 (친밀하거나 열정적인 신체 접촉)
expressions.nuance: 로맨틱하거나 성적인 뉘앙스를 가진 신체 접촉 표현. Rachel이 질투를 감추며 무심한 척 내뱉는 문장이라 더 감정적으로 읽힌다.
expressions.example: 드라마 속 클리셰 키스 장면을 묘사할 때 → 'He pressed her up against the wall and finally kissed her — total drama cliché, but I loved it.'

expressions.dialogue: Rach: He can press her up against that window as much as he wants. For all I care, he can throw her through the damn thing.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 내가 알 바 아니야, 나로서는 상관없어 (실제로는 매우 신경 쓰면서)
expressions.nuance: 냉담한 척하면서 실은 감정이 끓어오를 때 쓰는 표현. 아이러니가 강하게 깔린다. 'You can X for all I care' 패턴으로 자주 쓰인다.
expressions.example: 헤어진 후 전 남자친구 근황을 들었을 때 → 'He can date whoever he wants, for all I care. I've completely moved on.' (물론 그렇지 않을 수 있다)

expressions.dialogue:
Julie: Yeah, we figure it'll live with Ross half the time, and with me half the time.
Rach: Ohh, well, isn't that just lovely. That's something the two of you will be able to enjoy for a really, really, really, really, really long time.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: (반어적으로) 참 잘됐군요, 멋지네요
expressions.nuance: 표면상 칭찬이지만 실제로는 질투·불만을 숨긴 반어법. 목소리가 지나치게 달콤하거나 느리게 말할수록 반어의 강도가 세진다.
expressions.example: 싫어하는 동료가 또 좋은 자리를 차지했을 때 → 'Oh, isn't that just lovely. Of course he got the corner office.'

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: Uh, actually, we're getting a cat.
Rach: Together?
Ross: Uh huh.
Julie: Yeah, we figure it'll live with Ross half the time, and with me half the time.
expressions.speaker: Julie
expressions.meaning: ~하기로 생각하다, ~라고 계획하고 있다
expressions.nuance: 공식적으로 결정된 건 아니지만 그렇게 될 거라고 예상·계획하는 뉘앙스. 'think'나 'plan'보다 캐주얼하고 자연스럽다.
expressions.example: 여행 일정을 대충 잡았을 때 → 'We figure we'll spend two days in Busan and then head up to Seoul. Does that work for you?'

expressions.dialogue:
Mich: I've been through a divorce, trust me you're gonna be fine. You just can't see it now because you haven't had any closure.
Rach: Yeah! Closure. That's what it is, that's what I need. God, you're brilliant! Why didn't I think of that? How do I get that?
expressions.speaker: Michael / Rachel
expressions.meaning: 감정적 마무리, 관계나 상황에 대한 심리적 매듭짓기
expressions.nuance: 90년대 미국에서 심리치료 개념이 일상어로 정착한 단어. 'get closure', 'need closure', 'find closure'로 씀. 미련이나 해소되지 않은 감정이 남아있을 때 사용.
expressions.example: 갑자기 연락을 끊은 전 남자친구가 계속 생각날 때 → 'I never got closure after he just disappeared, and I think that's why I can't fully move on.'

expressions.dialogue: Mich: Well, you know, there's no one way really, it's just, you know, whatever it takes so that you can finally say to him, 'I'm over you.'
expressions.speaker: Michael
expressions.meaning: 필요한 수단이나 방법은 가리지 않고, 뭐든지 해서라도
expressions.nuance: 강한 결의와 수단에 대한 개방성을 동시에 표현. 'I'll do whatever it takes'처럼 단독으로도 강력한 선언이 된다.
expressions.example: 중요한 취업 면접을 앞두고 → 'I'll do whatever it takes to get this job — extra prep, mock interviews, everything.'

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: [listens to Rachel's voicemail] You're over me?
Rach: Ohhhhhhhh God.
Ross: Wha... you're uh, you're, you're over me? When, when were you... under me?
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: ~에 대한 감정이 정리됐다, 극복했다
expressions.nuance: 'Get over someone'이 극복하는 과정이라면 'be over someone'은 완료된 상태. Ross의 반응 'under me?'는 물리적 의미로 받아치는 언어유희.
expressions.example: 오래 짝사랑하다가 결국 포기하고 나서 → 'I think I'm finally over him. Ran into him last week and felt absolutely nothing.'

expressions.dialogue:
Chan: [weakly] No.
Mnca: Five more and I'll flash you.
...
Chan: Joey, be a pal. Lift up my hand and smack her with it.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 친구로서 부탁 하나만 들어줘
expressions.nuance: 'Do me a favor'보다 더 친근하고 장난기 있는 부탁 표현. 말도 안 되는 황당한 부탁 앞에 붙이면 유머 효과가 극대화된다.
expressions.example: 친구에게 황당한 부탁을 할 때 → 'Be a pal and just tell her I'm not here. I really can't deal with another lecture right now.'

expressions.expression: get in the way of something
expressions.dialogue:
Mnca: Ya wanna work out? I can remake you.
Chan: Oh, you know, I would, but that might get in the way of my lying around time.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: ~을 방해하다, ~에 지장을 주다
expressions.nuance: 'Interfere with'보다 캐주얼. 물리적 방해와 추상적 방해 모두 가능. 'Don't let X get in the way of Y' 패턴도 자주 씀.
expressions.example: 일이 취미 생활을 방해할 때 → 'Don't let work get in the way of what you actually enjoy. Burnout is real.'

expressions.dialogue:
Joey: Let me get this straight. He got you to beg to sleep with him, he got you to say he never has to call you again, and he got you thinking this was a great idea.
Phoe: Um-hum.
Joey: This man is my God.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 잠깐, 내가 제대로 이해한 건지 확인해보자
expressions.nuance: 믿기 어렵거나 황당한 상황을 요약·재확인할 때 씀. 말하는 사람의 놀라움이나 회의감이 내포되어 있다. 논쟁 상황에서 상대방 논리를 되짚을 때도 유용하다.
expressions.example: 친구가 황당한 상황을 설명했을 때 → 'Let me get this straight — he forgot your birthday, but you're the one who apologized?'

expressions.dialogue:
Phoe: And we did.
Joey: All right Phoebs, way to go.
Phoe: Yay me.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: 잘했어! 대단한데!
expressions.nuance: 진심 어린 칭찬부터 살짝 아이러니한 칭찬까지 톤에 따라 달라짐. 성취를 축하할 때 가장 간단하고 자연스러운 구어 표현 중 하나.
expressions.example: 오래 망설이다 드디어 상사에게 연봉 협상을 요청했을 때 → 'You finally asked for a raise? Way to go!'

expressions.dialogue:
Rach: Oh, you're not having fun, are you?
Mich: No, no, I am, but only because for the last hour and a half I've been playing the movie Diner in my head.
expressions.speaker: Michael
expressions.meaning: 머릿속으로 딴 생각을 하고 있었다, 상상 속으로 도피해 있었다
expressions.nuance: 지루하거나 불편한 상황에서 정신적으로 탈출하는 방식을 표현. 영화, 노래, 시나리오 등 무엇이든 올 수 있음. 상황의 지루함을 품위 있게 돌려 말하는 방법.
expressions.example: 2시간짜리 지루한 회의가 끝난 뒤 → 'I was basically just playing my whole vacation itinerary in my head for the last hour.'

expressions.dialogue:
Ross: The point is I don't need this right now, OK. It's too late, I'm with somebody else, I'm happy. This ship has sailed.
Rach: Yeah, what're you saying, you just sort of put away feelings or whatever the hell it was you felt for me?
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 기회는 이미 지나갔다, 이제 너무 늦었다
expressions.nuance: 돌이킬 수 없는 상황이나 지나간 기회를 표현하는 관용구. 단호하고 냉정한 어감. 'That ship has sailed'라고도 씀.
expressions.example: 인기 강의가 이미 마감됐을 때 → 'You should've signed up earlier — that ship has sailed. It sold out in ten minutes.'

expressions.dialogue:
Rach: Yeah, what're you saying, you just sort of put away feelings or whatever the hell it was you felt for me?
Ross: Hey, I've been doin' it since the ninth grade, I've gotten pretty damn good at it.
expressions.speaker: Rachel / Ross
expressions.meaning: 감정을 억누르다, 없는 것처럼 처리해버리다
expressions.nuance: 물건을 집어넣듯 감정을 '집어넣어 버린다'는 비유. 억눌러온 감정을 오랫동안 유지해온 사람의 고통이 담긴 표현으로, Ross의 대사에서 씁쓸함이 극대화된다.
expressions.example: 짝사랑하는 친구가 다른 사람과 사귀기 시작했을 때 → 'I had to just put away my feelings and be happy for her. It wasn't easy at all.'

expressions.expression: I've gotten pretty damn good at it
expressions.dialogue:
Rach: Yeah, what're you saying, you just sort of put away feelings or whatever the hell it was you felt for me?
Ross: Hey, I've been doin' it since the ninth grade, I've gotten pretty damn good at it.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 그 일을 꽤 잘하게 됐다 (오랜 경험으로)
expressions.nuance: 'Damn'이 강조사로 들어가 자조나 씁쓸함을 강화. 성취처럼 들리지만 실제로는 슬픈 현실을 드러내는 표현. 'pretty good'보다 감정이 훨씬 더 실려 있다.
expressions.example: 혼자 오래 살다 보니 어느새 살림을 잘하게 됐을 때 → 'I've been cooking for one for so long, I've gotten pretty damn good at making exactly enough for myself.'

expressions.expression: I am the energy train and you are on board
expressions.dialogue:
Chan: OK, stop.
Mnca: No, c'mon, we can't stop, c'mon, we've got three more pounds to go. I am the energy train and you are on board. Woo-woo, woo-woo, woo-woo.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 내가 이끄는 에너지 기관차야, 넌 그냥 올라타기만 하면 돼
expressions.nuance: Monica 특유의 강박적 열정을 보여주는 즉흥 비유. '멈추지 않는다', '방향이 정해져 있다'는 기관차의 속성으로 자신의 추진력을 과장. 이런 즉흥 비유를 쓰는 방식 자체가 미국식 코미디의 패턴이다.
expressions.example: 팀 프로젝트를 강하게 이끌 때 → 'I am the energy train on this project and we are not stopping until this ships. Everyone on board.'

expressions.dialogue: Mnca: We're workin', we're movin', we're in the zone we're groovin'.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.meaning: 최고의 집중 상태에 있다, 흐름을 완전히 타고 있다
expressions.nuance: 스포츠, 음악, 업무 등 어떤 활동에서든 쓸 수 있음. 방해를 받으면 'zone에서 나온다'는 느낌. 'get in the zone', 'break someone's zone' 등으로 응용 가능.
expressions.example: 코딩에 몰두하던 중 전화가 왔을 때 → 'I was totally in the zone and then my phone rang. Took me twenty minutes to get back into it.'

expressions.expression: I can't deal with this right now
expressions.dialogue:
Rach: Wait, so, you're going?
Ross: Well, OK, I uh, I have to. I can't deal with this right now. I mean, I've uh, y'know, I've got a cab, I've got a girlfriend, I'm... I'm gonna go get a cat.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.meaning: 지금 당장은 이 상황을 감당할 수 없다
expressions.nuance: 'Deal with'는 상황을 처리하다·감당하다는 뜻. 감정적으로 압도될 때 쓰는 솔직한 표현. 'right now'가 있어서 완전한 회피가 아닌 일시적 유예의 뉘앙스를 준다.
expressions.example: 갑자기 큰 결정을 요구받았을 때 → 'I can't deal with this right now — can we talk about it tomorrow when I've had time to think?'
